component.name: Primary-Secondary Buttons
component.description: Paired call-to-action row: primary (filled or outline) plus secondary outline. Layout is either full-width block buttons or compact inline buttons with optional leading icons. Labels and icons are instance overrides in Figma—not separate variants.

layoutSemantics.Inline: Compact horizontal pair (≈38px row height); primary and secondary support optional leading icons (e.g. check, close).
layoutSemantics.Block: Larger horizontal pair with generous padding; text-only primary and secondary (no icons in default Figma).

primaryToneSemantics.Success: Primary fill uses semantic success green; label example: MARK PAID.
primaryToneSemantics.Info: Primary fill uses semantic info blue; label examples: MARK IN PROGRESS, RESUME JOB (same tone—override text per screen).
primaryToneSemantics.Warning: Primary fill uses semantic warning orange; example: MARK COMPLETED.
primaryToneSemantics.Neutral: Primary fill uses foundation primary text color on white label; example: REOPEN JOB.
primaryToneSemantics.Outline: Primary is outline: white fill, default border, primary text; example: MARK UNPAID.

figmaLayerNames.componentSet: Primary-Secondary Buttons (vertical stack of variant masters, 16px gap)

codeProps.primaryTone: success, info, warning, neutral, or outline (matches Figma Primary)

notes: 2026-03-28: Removed duplicate Block+Info variant that only differed by label (RESUME JOB vs MARK IN PROGRESS); use Primary=Info with text overrides.
Renamed variants from Property 1 to Layout + Primary so the component set is valid.
Duplicate RESUME JOB row can be recreated as an instance of Layout=Block, Primary=Info with label override.

2026-04-08: `COMPONENT_SET` vertical auto layout (16px gap). Structural layers `primary-secondary-buttons-primary` / `secondary`; optional `primary-secondary-buttons-leading-icon` + label text nodes; `clipsContent` cleared on primary/secondary frames. Vectors left un-flattened in automation.
